widgetData.source_sentence: Given the claimant's allegations of delays to track linking, and the respondent's counter-arguments in RW-3, what specific documentation or data would be most probative in a preliminary assessment of the actual impact of incomplete blanketing work on the track installation timeline for CTP-1 and CTP-2?
sentences: 27.4 Waiver27.4.1 Waiver, including partial or conditional waiver, by either Party of any default by the other Party in the observance and performance of any provision of or obligations under this Agreement:-(a) shall not operate or be construed as a waiver of any other or subsequent default hereof or of other provisions of or obligations under this Agreement;(b) shall not be effective unless it is in writing and executed by a duly authorised representative of the Party; and(c) shall not affect the validity or enforceability of this Agreement in any manner.27.4.2 Neither the failure by either Party to insist on any occasion upon the performance of the terms, conditions and provisions of this Agreement or any obligation thereunder nor time or other indulgence granted by a Party to the other Party shall be treated or deemed as waiver of such breach or acceptance of any variation or the relinquishment of any such right hereunder.

APPLICABLE PERMITS1 Applicable Permits1.1 The Contractor shall obtain, as required under the Applicable Laws, the following Applicable Permits:(a) Permission of the State Government for extraction of boulders from quarry;(b) Permission of Village Panchayats and Pollution Control Board for installation of crushers;Licence for use of explosives;(d) Permission of the State Government for drawing water from river/reservoir;(e) Licence from inspector of factories or other competent Authority for setting up batching plant;Clearance of Pollution Control Board for setting up batching plant;(g) Clearance of Village Panchayats and Pollution Control Board for setting up asphalt plant;(h) Permission of Village Panchayats and State Government for borrow earth; and(i) Any other permits or clearances required under Applicable Laws.Applicable Permits, as required, relating to environmental protection and conservation shall have been procured by the Authority in accordance with the provisions of this Agreement.1.2morth/epc/nh/37010/4/2010180SCHEDULE - G (See Clauses 7.1.1, 7.5.3 and 19.2)

29. In paragraphs 104 to 111 of Mr. Kumar’s statement, Mr. Kumar reiterates various delays25 alleged by the Claimant with respect to track linking works. The Respondent has set out all the relevant facts26, correspondences, and analysis details concerning the track linking works. The issues related to track works are already explained in my Witness Statement RW-327. I submit my further observations below.30. In order to achieve Milestone 3, which encompasses the completion of track skeleton works for the entire Package, a series of activities must be executed for both CTP-1 and20 Appendix 10 Para No. 2.3 of ER21 Exhibit R-1 of the SOD (Part-2)22 Para No. 211 of the SOD23 Exhibit R-250 of the SOD24 Exhibit R-160 of the SOD25 Para Nos. 123 to 129 of the SOC26 Para Nos. 127 to 171 of the SOD27 Para 52 to 95 of Witness Statement of RW-3CTP-2. Among these activities, track linking/ installation stands out as a critical task in the Project. Specifically, it involves linking a total of 660 kilometres in CTP-1. The successful installation and linking of tracks depend on the completion of several crucial activities, as well as the availability of sufficient materials and equipment.Formation works in each section entail completing the earthwork up to the blanket layer. However, it is noteworthy that while sufficient embankment quantity has been completed in numerous sections, the blanketing work remains either uninitiated or has made minimal28 progress. The primary reasons for this are the inadequacy of resources, such as blanket material29 and the limited manpower mobilized at the Site. As a result, the completion of the blanket layer has been hindered, leading to delays in the overall Project progress.31.The track installation commenced on 24 September 2015 against the planned dates of 02 January 2015 but progressed over only two sections i.e., Bhagega to Shrimadhopur & Bhagega to Dabla until 31 August 2016. The reasons for the same were:32.

54. I would also like to highlight that there are certain obstructions such as temples which were not infringing the alignment and did not affect the formation works and track linking works. Thus, the Claimant’s assertion is denied in its entirety.55. Mr. Kumar reiterates that the CCP's sequence of events mandates the commencement of track skeleton works only after the completion of the formation works in the Bhagega-Dabla section. Also, the NTC was mobilized only on 24 September 2015, and thus, the statement of Mr. Kumar in paragraph 245 is denied in its entirety.56. However, it is important to note that this is not a contractual obligation, and track work could have started earlier without waiting for formation works for the entire section. The CCP can be revised based on actual progress and site conditions46. If there were any changes in the envisaged methodology as per the submitted CCP, the Claimant should have modified it accordingly. However, the Claimant did not undertake any such modifications to mitigate delays.57. The NTC was under frequent breakdowns and had produced an output of 800 meters per day against the output of 2.1 Km per day assumed in CCP, which is even lower than the NTC machine’s output recommended in the Harsco’s NTC brochure47.58. Even the Claimant has accepted48 that the NTC machine might be under breakdown from September 2015 to July 2016, however, it did not delay the time required for completion of MS-3 as it was not progressing as per the sequence.46 As specified in Para Nos. 246 and 247 of the SOD.47 As detailed in Para Nos. 155 to 159 of the SOD and Exhibit R-54, R-71, etc. of the SOD

46. The Contractor has not consented to the Engineer June 2021 Assessment and is aggrieved by the said assessment. The Contractor had notified its intention to commence DAB , vide letter SLT4/NKC/EMP- 4/CONT/EOT/2023/0958 dated 07.10.2023. The letter is enclosed as Exhibit-CD9.08.CO o 7-5 0> 26 | P a g eEMP-4 Dispute CD0947. There has been no determination by the Engineer, as contractually stipulated in terms of Clause 3.5 of the GC for the Contractor June 2021 Claims. Further, most of the delay events as on 30.06.2021 were continuing, such delay events had a significant effect on the progress of the Contractor's planned work. It is also relevant to note that on account of the continuing delays and the pending determination of the same by the Engineer, the Contractor has been facing severe disability with respect to effectively planning the remaining work under the Contract.48. It is submitted that withholding the determination of the Contractor June 2021 Claims is in clear violation of Clauses 20.1 read with Clauses 3.5 and 1.3 of the GCC. Clauses 1.3 and 3.5 of the GCC are set out here under:

sentences: their service provider in absence of M/S TCIL” 242 C-107, SLT’s letter no. 4054 114 211. In the 18 August 2020 Letter, SLT again “reserve[d] his [sic] rights to claim variation arose [sic] due to SA-03, under clause 3.3, 3.5, 14 and all clauses relevant in [the Contract].”243 212. Unsurprisingly, the Engineer rejected SLT’s proposed revisions in its letter dated 24 August 2020 (the “24 August 2020 Letter”), and insisted on its inaccurate account of the factual events.244 213. I should highlight that SLT was effectively backed into a corner by DFCC into executing the supplementary agreement. This was because Vista was neither the stipulated Specialist Sub-Contractor under the Contract (like Huawei was under paragraph 6 of the Contract) nor one of the other pre- approved Specialist Sub-Contractors under the Contract (like Nokia and TCIL were). Proceeding to engage Vista without formally incorporating Vista as the Specialist Sub-Contractor in place of Huawei and TCIL would therefore cause SLT to run the risk of breaching the Contract for engaging an unapproved specialist sub-contractor. In addition, DFCC was slow in making its progress payments to SLT in relation to the GSM-R works until the supplementary agreement was finalised and executed. 214. On 10 March 2022, SLT issued the final version, SA-04 (i.e. the Supplementary Agreement), for DFCC’s execution.245 It is clear from the 243 Ibid 244 C-108, PMC’s letter no. 39128 245 C-120, SLT’s letter no. 6127, pages 17 - 27 115 correspondence that I have highlighted above SLT had effectively been compelled to adopt the Engineer’s/ DFCC’s contrived account of the factual events, and any reference or inference to the Engineer’s/ DFCC’s unwarranted rejection of Huawei and Nokia (as was the case in reality) was excluded. SLT was also prohibited from including the reservation of rights clause in the Supplementary Agreement. Nevertheless, Recital H of the

2.1.1.3. The Contractor claims to have numerous written letters on the subject of encumbrance free access to the site as provided for in the Contract Agreement. In correspondence between May to June 2015 the use of a joint statement signed by the Engineer's and Contractor's representatives recording encumbrances present was established. At a meeting on 7 August 2015(NKC-DFCC-1508-43 dated 17-august-2015, mom held on 07.aug.2015) , the Engineer instructed the Contractor to take over all SCP locations with encumbrances to be listed and duly signed off at joint inspections.As Delay Event No 6 addresses the issue of late handover of SCP site to the Contractor for MS2, the same issues and arguments as proposed by the Contractor for MSI in Delay Event No 1 will apply and except that it covers different site areas/location.2.1.1.4. The contradictions and unknown relative weightings of these clauses demonstrate some mismanagement of STP-17 by DFCC, and have ultimately led to this dispute. (b)

Interoperability Tests between NOKIA NSS and Huawei BSS GSM-R Expert Statement dated 3 July 2015. (ix) Attachment 7: TuV Certificate ISV mod SB directive 2008-57- EC. (x) Attachment 8: TuV Certificate ISV mod SB directive 2008-57- EC. (xi) Attachment 9: TuV EC Type Examination Report LC_B14_432. ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 26 (xii) Attachment 10: TuV Technical File LC_B14_433-TD. (b) As mentioned above, this letter contained Huawei’s GSM-R Compliance Statement which lists the relevant GSM-R requirement clauses, as extracted from STP-17 ER:PS Clause 6, together with supporting

consultations done with other GSM-R experts, we have identified the followings observations during the assessment process. 6.1 GSM-R Interoperability declaration restricted to territory of Poland ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 98 Comment: The restriction does not have any validity on the Technical performance of the I0T if the same equipment, architecture and backward compatible software version is deployed in any other country. 6.2 Interoperability restrictions between KCC core and Nokia Access which are of no concern in Poland, has to be evaluated against Indian operational requirements. Comment: The restrictions related to ETSI Features (S. No. I, Page 2, Page3 of "Annexure E") are for those ETSI features which are not part of ERIENE specifications and hence these features are not relevant for GSM-R network. The QoS required for a particular speed of operation (e.g. WDFC2 requires for 220 km/h) is related to Radio Network (BSS) functionality. The Radio network planning needs to ensure that GSM-R network will provide the required QoS to enable the required speed of Railway Operation. The Radio networking planning, QoS and Speed of Train operation is not dependent on NSS network functionality. It is to be ensured during RF design that the planning is done in a way that speeds up to 220 km/h will not impact the performance of the radio network. (e) Systra’s report later provides the following specific conclusions about the test results: “The IOT testing between Nokia Solutions and Networks • BSS/Radio architecture: FlexiBSC/TCSM3i/FlexiBTS; SW: RGR20 and Kapsch CarrierCom GSM-R architecture • BSS: BSC3000, TCU3000, BTS9000, SW: BSSv18 • NSS: R4 ATCA, SW: NSS20 ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 99 was successfully completed during March and April 2014; all planned test cases were performed. The IOT tests were monitored by independent rail organization:

ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 123 that he is unable to recognize the pre-bid clarification. This is against the spirit of the contract. ... We refer to clause no. 6.5.1.1 (2) of part 3A of STP-17 contract and the same clause no. 6.5.1.1 (2) of part 3A (issued vide amendment no. 8) of STP-5 contract. They read as follows. "MSSs of WDFC (provided by STP-5) shall be interoperable with BSC(s) of Indian Railways. MSSs of WDFCC shall be interoperable with BSC being procured for Khuraja-Bhaupur —Mughal Sarai section of EDFC and vice versa". ... Kapsch has not approached Nokia for any such joint declaration ... This is despite the fact (above contract clause no. [i.e. 6.5.1.1] mentioned in both STP-5 & STP-17) that the Kapsch MSS approved for STP-5 should be interoperable with the following 4 categories of BSCs. i) with all the Nokia's BSCs deployed in Indian Railways (IR), ii) with the Nokia's Flexi BSC deployed in metro railway of Kolkata # which is part of IR, iii) with the Nokia's Flexi BSC under implementation in CP104 (EDFC) and iv) with the BSC that would be implemented in CP203 (EDFC). In view of the above, we again request you to kindly arrange to accord approval of "Nokia" as specialist subcontractor for GSM-R works for STP-17.” (b) In summary: ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 124 (i) In the pre-bid clarifications, the Employer previously allowed integration of BTS(s) of Phase 2 (STP-17) with BSC(s) etc. of Phase 1 (STP-5). (ii) The Engineer then reneged on the Employer’s pre-bid clarifications saying he was then unable to recognize the pre-bid clarification. (iii) The STP-5 contract already allowed for interoperability of MSCs in STP-5 with Nokia BSCs for IR (India Railways). (iv) Based on the above information, SLT requested that DFCC approve its Nokia BSS proposal. (c) My comments and opinions on this are: (i)

widgetData.source_sentence: Given that 66 out of 79 TSS/SCP sites were encumbrance-free by August 31, 2016, what potential liabilities arise if the contractor argues that these specific encumbrances, despite their temporary nature, fundamentally altered the project's critical path and increased overall costs?
sentences: To conclude and with reference to above sub-paras under 2.1.2, it is stated that out of 79 TSS/SCPs sites in MS-1 and MS-2 section, 66 TSS/SCPs sites were encumbrance free as on 31.08.2016. Regarding encumbrances on the remaining TSS/SCP sites, these were mostly issue of interface management with civil and track Contractor mainly for removal of their material which is temporarily stacked at site. However, even on these sites, which were available to the contractor and are encumbrance free as on 31.08.2016, the progress of work was nil for any of the CCP activities such as ( site survey, clearing and grubbing, site preparatory works, earthwork etc. which are preceding activities to construction activity ). (Engineer's letter dated 03.10.2017 & 02.07.2018 at SN 53 & 54,). As such from above. it is clear that non availability of the encumbrance free sites to the contractor is not a reason for contractor's non- commencement of the work and non-achievement of MS-1 & MS-2 or cause for any delay.The Contractor has not substantiated as to how non handing over of SCP sites or individual obstructions at these sites prevented him for proceeding with Permanent Work and affected / delayed the permanent work. The Contractor has not established the impact of the encumbered conditions to the time for completion and hence, it is clear that non availability of the encumbrance free sites to the contractor is not a reason for contractor's non- commencement of the work/ non-achievement of MS-1 & MS-2 or any cause of delay.In addition to above, Contractor's attention is invited to Engineer's letters on the subject of SLOW Progress of the Contractor letter cited at SN 55, SN56, SN57, SN58, SN59 (for delays accrued up to 30.06.2021), which gives details of item wise deficiency /shortfall in progress of various works of the SLT4 Contractor for reasons entirely attributable to the Contractor.

and SN-6 of Addendum 7) of the contract states that - "All civil works or modifications required for installation of the equipment and restoring to final finishes. This shall include but not limited to preparation and leveling of ground, ground investigation, hydrological studies, earth filling to lift the land to obtain the lowest point of the cable trench 0.5 m above the highest flood level, construction of the building, cable trenches, access roads, fences, drainage, OHE and other foundations, traction equipment/ component foundations and containments. If HFL is not available with Local authorities/ Irrigation Department, the bidder will obtain such information for nearest water bodies,for this purpose from the appropriate authority." the SCPs. which were given NONOC on 16-02-2016. Subsequent submissions were made on 26- 02-2016 (NONOC given on 29-02-2016), 02-03-2016 (NONOC given on 04-03-2016), 11-03-2016 (NONOC on 18-03-2016) 14-04-2016 & 21-04-2016 ( NONOC on 16-05-2016). It is evident from the above that there is no delay by Engineer in issuing NONO/NONOC to the submissions made by the contractor. However, during joint inspection with the contractor On 01-04-2016 & 04-06-2016, it was noted by the Engineer that Contractors drawings were not as per site conditions, and Contractor did not possess authentic details for HFL at several locations ( Engineer's letters dated 02-06-2016 and 06-06-2016). for

“5.2 Functional and technical specifications 5.2.1 The comparison of functional and technical specifications "Annexure G" (EIRENE Functionality Comparison between Poland Network and STP-17 contract) has been provided which compares the requirement in India DFCC project and what was tested / deployed in Poland project. 5.2.2 As per WDFC contract, vendor has to deploy GSM-R network compliant with EIRENE FRS v7.4.0/SRSv15.4.0. 5.2.3 Moreover, it has been observed that the WDFC technical specification is based on EIRENE FRS/SRS. 76 C-060 77 C-059 ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 97 Both optional specification and mandatory specification are the same in Poland project and in STP-17 project. 5.3 Hardware & software levels of all Equipment 5.3.1 Based on document on "IOT Declaration/IOT Certificate related to Poland Network" (Annexure A, Annexure C) and Nokia proposal for WDFC2 network, it is understood that same hardware version will supplied by Nokia as is used in Poland Network. 5.3.2 The interoperability testing performed in Poland between KCC and Nokia have been evaluated between the KCC Core and Nokia Flexi BSS. (Annexure A, Annexure C) 5.3.3 Additional interoperability testing performed between KCC NSS Nokia Flexi BSS has been evaluated. (Annexure B - GSM-R IOT Declaration No. 2 by KCC and Nokia) Observation 1: The interfacing between Nokia BSS and KCC NSS is limited to "A" interface which is an open interface defined by 3GPP.” (d) Systra’s report p. 12 provides the following general conclusions: “6. CONCLUSION The analysis of the different certificates and documents presented for consultation to SYSTRA has enabled to assess the compatibility between KCC Core and Nokia Access network for the Indian GSM- R project. In the 18 August 2020 Letter, SLT again “reserve[d] his [sic] rights to claim variation arose [sic] due to SA-03, under clause 3.3, 3.5, 14 and all clauses relevant in [the Contract].”243 212. Unsurprisingly, the Engineer rejected SLT’s proposed revisions in its letter dated 24 August 2020 (the “24 August 2020 Letter”), and insisted on its inaccurate account of the factual events.244 213. I should highlight that SLT was effectively backed into a corner by DFCC into executing the supplementary agreement. This was because Vista was neither the stipulated Specialist Sub-Contractor under the Contract (like Huawei was under paragraph 6 of the Contract) nor one of the other pre- approved Specialist Sub-Contractors under the Contract (like Nokia and TCIL were). Proceeding to engage Vista without formally incorporating Vista as the Specialist Sub-Contractor in place of Huawei and TCIL would therefore cause SLT to run the risk of breaching the Contract for engaging an unapproved specialist sub-contractor. In addition, DFCC was slow in making its progress payments to SLT in relation to the GSM-R works until the supplementary agreement was finalised and executed. 214. On 10 March 2022, SLT issued the final version, SA-04 (i.e. the Supplementary Agreement), for DFCC’s execution.245 It is clear from the 243 Ibid 244 C-108, PMC’s letter no. 39128 245 C-120, SLT’s letter no. 6127, pages 17 - 27 115 correspondence that I have highlighted above SLT had effectively been compelled to adopt the Engineer’s/ DFCC’s contrived account of the factual events, and any reference or inference to the Engineer’s/ DFCC’s unwarranted rejection of Huawei and Nokia (as was the case in reality) was excluded. SLT was also prohibited from including the reservation of rights clause in the Supplementary Agreement. Nevertheless, Recital H of the

2.1.1.3. The Contractor claims to have numerous written letters on the subject of encumbrance free access to the site as provided for in the Contract Agreement. In correspondence between May to June 2015 the use of a joint statement signed by the Engineer's and Contractor's representatives recording encumbrances present was established. At a meeting on 7 August 2015(NKC-DFCC-1508-43 dated 17-august-2015, mom held on 07.aug.2015) , the Engineer instructed the Contractor to take over all SCP locations with encumbrances to be listed and duly signed off at joint inspections.As Delay Event No 6 addresses the issue of late handover of SCP site to the Contractor for MS2, the same issues and arguments as proposed by the Contractor for MSI in Delay Event No 1 will apply and except that it covers different site areas/location.2.1.1.4. Conclusion on compatibility of Nokia’s solution ........................................... 124 3 CONCLUSION .................................................................................................... 126 4 DECLARATION .................................................................................................... 127 5 GLOSSARY ......................................................................................................... 128 Appendix 1 Curriculum Vitae of Dr Boyd Murray .......................................................... 132 ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 3 I, DR BOYD MURRAY, care of Murray Wireless., LLC, 9 Bedford Road, North Epping NSW 2121, do solemnly and sincerely swear / affirm and say as follows: 1 INTRODUCTION 1.1 Executive Summary 1.1.1 I, Dr Boyd Murray, am an electrical/telecommunications engineer with over 45 years of experience in the electronics, avionics, and telecommunications industries. 1.1.2 It is my opinion that: (a) The ER:PS (Employer Requirements Particular Specifications) had contradictions in several of its clauses regarding the allowance of an additional BSC (Base Station Controller), from a different equipment OEM, in STP-17 for interfacing to the MSC (Mobile Switching Centre) in STP-5. The contradictions and unknown relative weightings of these clauses demonstrate some mismanagement of STP-17 by DFCC, and have ultimately led to this dispute. (b)

consultations done with other GSM-R experts, we have identified the followings observations during the assessment process. 6.1 GSM-R Interoperability declaration restricted to territory of Poland ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 98 Comment: The restriction does not have any validity on the Technical performance of the I0T if the same equipment, architecture and backward compatible software version is deployed in any other country. 6.2 Interoperability restrictions between KCC core and Nokia Access which are of no concern in Poland, has to be evaluated against Indian operational requirements. Comment: The restrictions related to ETSI Features (S. No. I, Page 2, Page3 of "Annexure E") are for those ETSI features which are not part of ERIENE specifications and hence these features are not relevant for GSM-R network. The QoS required for a particular speed of operation (e.g. WDFC2 requires for 220 km/h) is related to Radio Network (BSS) functionality. The Radio network planning needs to ensure that GSM-R network will provide the required QoS to enable the required speed of Railway Operation. The Radio networking planning, QoS and Speed of Train operation is not dependent on NSS network functionality. It is to be ensured during RF design that the planning is done in a way that speeds up to 220 km/h will not impact the performance of the radio network. The IOT tests were monitored by independent rail organization:

ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 123 that he is unable to recognize the pre-bid clarification. This is against the spirit of the contract. ... We refer to clause no. 6.5.1.1 (2) of part 3A of STP-17 contract and the same clause no. 6.5.1.1 (2) of part 3A (issued vide amendment no. 8) of STP-5 contract. They read as follows. "MSSs of WDFC (provided by STP-5) shall be interoperable with BSC(s) of Indian Railways. MSSs of WDFCC shall be interoperable with BSC being procured for Khuraja-Bhaupur —Mughal Sarai section of EDFC and vice versa". ... Kapsch has not approached Nokia for any such joint declaration ... This is despite the fact (above contract clause no. [i.e. 6.5.1.1] mentioned in both STP-5 & STP-17) that the Kapsch MSS approved for STP-5 should be interoperable with the following 4 categories of BSCs. i) with all the Nokia's BSCs deployed in Indian Railways (IR), ii) with the Nokia's Flexi BSC deployed in metro railway of Kolkata # which is part of IR, iii) with the Nokia's Flexi BSC under implementation in CP104 (EDFC) and iv) with the BSC that would be implemented in CP203 (EDFC). In view of the above, we again request you to kindly arrange to accord approval of "Nokia" as specialist subcontractor for GSM-R works for STP-17.” (b) In summary: ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 124 (i) In the pre-bid clarifications, the Employer previously allowed integration of BTS(s) of Phase 2 (STP-17) with BSC(s) etc. of Phase 1 (STP-5). (ii) The Engineer then reneged on the Employer’s pre-bid clarifications saying he was then unable to recognize the pre-bid clarification. (iii) The STP-5 contract already allowed for interoperability of MSCs in STP-5 with Nokia BSCs for IR (India Railways). (iv) Based on the above information, SLT requested that DFCC approve its Nokia BSS proposal. (c) My comments and opinions on this are: (i)

widget.source_sentence: Given that 66 out of 79 TSS/SCP sites were encumbrance-free by August 31, 2016, what potential liabilities arise if the contractor argues that these specific encumbrances, despite their temporary nature, fundamentally altered the project's critical path and increased overall costs?
sentences: To conclude and with reference to above sub-paras under 2.1.2, it is stated that out of 79 TSS/SCPs sites in MS-1 and MS-2 section, 66 TSS/SCPs sites were encumbrance free as on 31.08.2016. Regarding encumbrances on the remaining TSS/SCP sites, these were mostly issue of interface management with civil and track Contractor mainly for removal of their material which is temporarily stacked at site. However, even on these sites, which were available to the contractor and are encumbrance free as on 31.08.2016, the progress of work was nil for any of the CCP activities such as ( site survey, clearing and grubbing, site preparatory works, earthwork etc. which are preceding activities to construction activity ). (Engineer's letter dated 03.10.2017 & 02.07.2018 at SN 53 & 54,). As such from above. it is clear that non availability of the encumbrance free sites to the contractor is not a reason for contractor's non- commencement of the work and non-achievement of MS-1 & MS-2 or cause for any delay.The Contractor has not substantiated as to how non handing over of SCP sites or individual obstructions at these sites prevented him for proceeding with Permanent Work and affected / delayed the permanent work. The Contractor has not established the impact of the encumbered conditions to the time for completion and hence, it is clear that non availability of the encumbrance free sites to the contractor is not a reason for contractor's non- commencement of the work/ non-achievement of MS-1 & MS-2 or any cause of delay.In addition to above, Contractor's attention is invited to Engineer's letters on the subject of SLOW Progress of the Contractor letter cited at SN 55, SN56, SN57, SN58, SN59 (for delays accrued up to 30.06.2021), which gives details of item wise deficiency /shortfall in progress of various works of the SLT4 Contractor for reasons entirely attributable to the Contractor.

and SN-6 of Addendum 7) of the contract states that - "All civil works or modifications required for installation of the equipment and restoring to final finishes. This shall include but not limited to preparation and leveling of ground, ground investigation, hydrological studies, earth filling to lift the land to obtain the lowest point of the cable trench 0.5 m above the highest flood level, construction of the building, cable trenches, access roads, fences, drainage, OHE and other foundations, traction equipment/ component foundations and containments. If HFL is not available with Local authorities/ Irrigation Department, the bidder will obtain such information for nearest water bodies,for this purpose from the appropriate authority." the SCPs. which were given NONOC on 16-02-2016. Subsequent submissions were made on 26- 02-2016 (NONOC given on 29-02-2016), 02-03-2016 (NONOC given on 04-03-2016), 11-03-2016 (NONOC on 18-03-2016) 14-04-2016 & 21-04-2016 ( NONOC on 16-05-2016). It is evident from the above that there is no delay by Engineer in issuing NONO/NONOC to the submissions made by the contractor. “5.2 Functional and technical specifications 5.2.1 The comparison of functional and technical specifications "Annexure G" (EIRENE Functionality Comparison between Poland Network and STP-17 contract) has been provided which compares the requirement in India DFCC project and what was tested / deployed in Poland project. 5.2.2 As per WDFC contract, vendor has to deploy GSM-R network compliant with EIRENE FRS v7.4.0/SRSv15.4.0. 5.2.3 Moreover, it has been observed that the WDFC technical specification is based on EIRENE FRS/SRS. 76 C-060 77 C-059 ICC Case No. 28100/HTG EXPERT REPORT OF DR BOYD MURRAY PAGE 97 Both optional specification and mandatory specification are the same in Poland project and in STP-17 project. 5.3 Hardware & software levels of all Equipment 5.3.1 Based on document on "IOT Declaration/IOT Certificate related to Poland Network" (Annexure A, Annexure C) and Nokia proposal for WDFC2 network, it is understood that same hardware version will supplied by Nokia as is used in Poland Network. 5.3.2 The interoperability testing performed in Poland between KCC and Nokia have been evaluated between the KCC Core and Nokia Flexi BSS. (Annexure A, Annexure C) 5.3.3 Additional interoperability testing performed between KCC NSS Nokia Flexi BSS has been evaluated. (Annexure B - GSM-R IOT Declaration No. 2 by KCC and Nokia) Observation 1: The interfacing between Nokia BSS and KCC NSS is limited to "A" interface which is an open interface defined by 3GPP.” (d) Systra’s report p. 12 provides the following general conclusions: “6. CONCLUSION The analysis of the different certificates and documents presented for consultation to SYSTRA has enabled to assess the compatibility between KCC Core and Nokia Access network for the Indian GSM- R project.
